problems.cause: THROT.Actual is an internal subcomponent port with type incompatible with Actual_Speed (Types::speed.speed_impl); AGREE annex references Actual_Speed.val which is valid, but AADL connection type mismatch breaks model validity.
problems.minimal_edit: Remove the AGREE annex from Car (component type) and move it to Car.Car_Impl (implementation) to allow use of implementation-level port bindings as context.

problems.diagnostic: /CaseProject/modified_model.aadl | line 34: The types of 'Target_Speed' and 'Target' do not match.
problems.cause: Target_Speed (Types::speed.speed_impl) is connected to CNTRL.Target, whose type is not declared as compatible; AGREE assumes Target_Speed.val is valid, but AADL type mismatch invalidates the connection.
problems.minimal_edit: Remove the AGREE annex from Car (component type) and move it to Car.Car_Impl (implementation) to allow use of implementation-level port bindings as context.

problems.cause: SM.State_Out type is incompatible with State_Signal (Types::state_sig.impl); AGREE annex does not reference State_Out but the model is invalid, requiring annex relocation to implementation context where the connection is resolved.
problems.minimal_edit: Remove the AGREE annex from Car (component type) and move it to Car.Car_Impl (implementation) to allow use of implementation-level port bindings as context.

executor_steps: Delete the current annex agree block from the Car component type (lines 13-19).
Add a new annex agree block to the Car.Car_Impl implementation scope, immediately after the connections block.
In the new implementation-level annex, retain all original assume/guarantee clauses but ensure all port references (Target_Speed.val, Actual_Speed.val) are valid under implementation context.
Do not modify any AADL port types or connections — the repair is purely relocating the annex to a context where the connections are already declared and validated.
